title: Why is it recommended to run only one process in a container?
description: In the Docker document recommends the use of one service per container for separation of areas of interest as a best practice. In addition, many do...

children.value: It’s best practice to separate areas of concern by using one service per container.

for separation of areas of interest as a best practice.
In addition, many documents online mention this a lot, so why encourage the use of one service per container?
Benefits of running a single service per container
: It makes it much easier to scale specific services as needed.

If multi-services are operated in one container, even the same service may require different settings for each container when horizontally expanding.
In this case, it may be more complicated than when the docker is not used.
: The service becomes much easier to reuse in other projects that require it.
If multi-services were run in one container, they would not be available in other projects because they are specialized in the project.
: Since the scope of work reduced, it becomes much easier to debug security patches or errors.

If multi-services are operated in one container, other non-patch-related services in the container may also be affected when applying security patches due to the high degree of combination between services.
Therefore, it may be necessary to perform tasks that would not have been necessary for a single service per container configuration. This means that portability is low, and predictability is also low because it is relatively difficult to predict the range of effects of errors.
Disadvantages of running multi-service per container

: If multi-services are run in one container, it is difficult to identify which service is output from.
Therefore, additional settings should be made to enable identification, or logs should be redirected to a separate file.

: Docker performs container health checks with PID 1.
Therefore, if PID 1 is normal when driving multi-services, it may not be detected even if the rest of the process is terminated due to an error.

It was confirmed that it was better to configure a container with a single service due to the advantages of single service per container + the disadvantages of multi-service  per container.
One thing that I regret is that I wanted to check what would happen if there was an exception, but I couldn't record it because there were no significant examples. If I find a good example later, I will add it.
Containers: one single process, or multiple processes?
Why it is recommended to run only one process in a container?

links.text: Benefits of running a single service per container
Disadvantages of running multi-service per container

title: Extracting a list of time zones with Java +Kotlin
description: I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted. Therefore...

children.value:
I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted.
Therefore, processing was necessary, but I wanted to process it into a code so that it could be easily managed in the future.
I remember seeing the API that brings up the list of zones on the time package side, so when I looked it up, I found
, and I succeeded in extracting the list using it, so I summarized it.
The reason for using replace for offset is that if it is +-0, it is written as

like other offsets.
Therefore, it was used to unify the form.
Without sorting, it is randomly mixed, not in name order or offset order. Therefore, I ordered it in ascending order based on offset.
It's not that different, but I've also written Kotlin code.
